output:
Implemented T5: `TimelineEditor` now destructures `updateToLatestVideo`, wraps it in a local handler, passes `staleShotGroupIds` as a typed prop, and forwards the new update callback into `TimelineCanvas`, which now passes it through to `ShotGroupContextMenu`. Checkpointed batch progress to `.megaplan/plans/unify-shot-group-sync-into-a-20260406-1746/execution_batch_3.json`.

Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` passed. The full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module still shows the same pre-existing pinned-shot expectation failures from earlier batches (`data-shot-group-kind` assertions and stale `Unpin` label expectations).
deviations: The full `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module still fails on pre-existing pinned-shot assertions unrelated to this callback-threading batch: missing `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` expectations and stale `Unpin` menu-label expectations.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified `TimelineEditor` now destructures `updateToLatestVideo`, wraps it in a local callback that mirrors the existing final-video switch flow, and passes both that callback and the typed `staleShotGroupIds` prop into `TimelineCanvas`. Verified `TimelineCanvas` forwards `onShotGroupUpdateToLatestVideo` directly into `ShotGroupContextMenu`, preserving the `{ shotId, rowId }` payload through the full component chain.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The new update callback follows the same `TimelineEditor -> TimelineCanvas -> ShotGroupContextMenu` threading pattern already used for `onSwitchToFinalVideo` and `onSwitchToImages`, and `TimelineCanvas` now forwards the callback without reshaping or dropping its `{ shotId, rowId }` payload.
